steps.title: Identify what 'superior' means in this context
steps.content:
Snowball sampling may be superior—which means more effective at recruitment.

Since both methods are tested for their ability to recruit participants with the desired characteristics, evidence of superiority would show snowball sampling outperforming purposive sampling at recruiting such participants.

steps.title: Find evidence of snowball's recruitment advantage
steps.content:
Choice C provides direct evidence: snowball sampling yielded significantly more participants with the desired characteristics than purposive sampling did.

The other choices either fail to compare the two methods, suggest snowball selected wrong participants, or focus on irrelevant attributes.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
